results.name: iShares Bitcoin ETF
keyThemes: ETF inflow rebound after prolonged outflow streak
Institutional accumulation led by BlackRock IBIT
Options activity and long-dated calls interest
Bitcoin macro momentum and 200-week MA support
Dominant trading volume concentration in IBIT
results.topBullish:
Crypto ETF Inflows Rebound

On July 10, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $90.4M in net inflows, led by BlackRock's IBIT with $86.8M. Spot Ethereum ETFs also added $18.4M. The rebound marked Bitcoin ETFs' first weekly net inflow since May.

#BTC #BitcoinETF #EthereumETF #IBIT
results.topBearish: The 8-week ETF outflow run is over, though the print flatters: strip out $IBIT and the complex was still negative. One desk turning off its sell program
results.summary: IBIT sentiment is broadly bullish, driven by confirmed institutional inflow recovery — $86.8M on July 10 alone — ending an extended outflow streak and signaling renewed institutional conviction in spot Bitcoin exposure. Trading volume dominance ($1.11B of $1.31B total Bitcoin ETF volume), active options interest including long-dated calls, and technical support at the 200-week moving average reinforce a constructive near-term setup, though one cautionary note flags that stripping out IBIT the broader crypto ETF complex remains net negative, suggesting concentration risk in a single fund.
